title: grewia serratula
intro: Grewia serratula, also known as the wild guelder or Indian plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to tropical Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Grewia serratula include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
